DESCRIPTOR.LABEL: ENA marker sequence checklist
DESCRIPTOR.NAME: ena_marker_sequence_checklist
DESCRIPTOR.DESCRIPTION: This is general checklist for barcode marker sequence data. It is used for automatic sequence and metadata feeds from BOLD for example.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Collection event information

FIELD.LABEL: collection date
FIELD.NAME: collection_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The date the sample was collected with the intention of sequencing, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[12][0-9]{3}(-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])(-(0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?(/[0-9]{4}(-[0-9]{2}(-[0-9]{2}(T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}(:[0-9]{2})?Z?([+-][0-9]{1,2})?)?)?)?)?$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (region and locality)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_region_and_locality
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the specific region name followed by the locality name.

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (longitude)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_longitude
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (latitude)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_latitude
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees and in WGS84 system

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: (^[+-]?[0-9]+.?[0-9]{0,8}$)|(^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$)

FIELD.LABEL: collected_by
FIELD.NAME: collected_by
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: name of persons or institute who collected the specimen

FIELD.LABEL: elevation
FIELD.NAME: elevation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The elevation of the sampling site as measured by the vertical distance from mean sea level.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: ([+-]?(0|((0\.)|([1-9][0-9]*\.?))[0-9]*)([Ee][+-]?[0-9]+)?)|((^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$))

FIELD.LABEL: altitude
FIELD.NAME: altitude
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The altitude of the sample is the vertical distance between Earth's surface above Sea Level and the sampled position in the air.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: ((0|((0\.)|([1-9][0-9]*\.?))[0-9]*)([Ee][+-]?[0-9]+)?)|((^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$))

FIELD.LABEL: identified_by
FIELD.NAME: identified_by
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: name of the expert who identified the specimen taxonomically

FIELD.LABEL: broad-scale environmental context
FIELD.NAME: broadscale_environmental_context
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the major environmental system the sample or specimen came from. The system(s) identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. in the desert or a rainforest). We recommend using subclasses of EnvO’s biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

FIELD.LABEL: local environmental context
FIELD.NAME: local_environmental_context
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen’s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for "broad-scale environmental context". Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS.

FIELD.LABEL: identifier_affiliation
FIELD.NAME: identifier_affiliation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: the university, institution, or society responsible for identifying the specimen.

FIELD.LABEL: environmental medium
FIELD.NAME: environmental_medium
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top).

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Organism characteristics

FIELD.LABEL: geographic location (country and/or sea)
FIELD.NAME: geographic_location_country_andor_sea
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The geographical origin of where the sample was collected from, with the intention of sequencing, as defined by the country or sea name. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html).

TEXT_VALUE.VALUE: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

FIELD.LABEL: predicted taxon id
FIELD.NAME: predicted_taxon_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The most likely Taxonomy ID of the organism as in the NCBI Taxonomy database. Entries in the NCBI Taxonomy database have integer taxon IDs. Only to use when the organism identification is uncertain.

FIELD.LABEL: taxonomic identification method
FIELD.NAME: taxonomic_identification_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The method used to identify the specimen to nominal species (e.g. morphology, DNA barcoding). Should include reference to an authoritative key if possible. { examples: ["morphology"] }

FIELD.LABEL: taxonomic identification remarks
FIELD.NAME: taxonomic_identification_remarks
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: General notes regarding the taxonomy of the specimen.

FIELD.LABEL: sex
FIELD.NAME: sex
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: sex of the organism from which the sample was obtained

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection: methods, storage and transport

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection device
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_device
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The device used to collect an environmental sample. It is recommended to use terms listed under environmental sampling device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO) and/or terms listed under specimen collection device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0002094).

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection device solution
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_device_solution
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Suspension liquid used in the collection device (e.g. 100% Ethanol).

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection device direction
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_device_direction
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Direction of the device used for sample collection reported as cardinal (north, south, east, west) or intercardinal (northeast, southeast, northwest, southwest) directions (e.g. direction of the bottle end in Malaise traps).

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection method
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_method
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The method employed for collecting the sample. Can be provided in the form of a PMID, DOI, url or text.

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage conditions
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_conditions
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Conditions at which sample was stored, usually storage temperature, duration and location. In soil context: Explain how and for how long the soil sample was stored before DNA extraction (fresh/frozen/other).

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage device
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_device
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The container used to store the sample. This field accepts terms listed under container (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C43186). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the storage device.

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage location
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_location
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Location at which sample was stored, usually name of a specific freezer/room. Indicate the location name.

FIELD.LABEL: sample storage temperature
FIELD.NAME: sample_storage_temperature
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: temperature at which sample was stored, e.g. -80

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: General collection event information

FIELD.LABEL: sample composed of
FIELD.NAME: sample_composed_of
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reference to sample(s) that are part of a sample, for example when samples are pooled for analysis or when a group of samples corresponds to a higher level sample. The referenced sample(s) should already be registered in INSDC. This should be formatted as one of the following. A single sample e.g. ERSxxxxxx OR a comma separated list e.g. ERSxxxxxx,ERSxxxxxx

FIELD.LABEL: sample collection batch id
FIELD.NAME: sample_collection_batch_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Unique identifier assigned to the sample collection container. Only applicable for batch sample collection (e.g. insect collection in Malaise traps). { examples: ["UK2654"] }

FIELD.LABEL: collecting institution
FIELD.NAME: collecting_institution
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the institution to which the person collecting the specimen belongs. Format: Institute Name, Institute Address

FIELD.LABEL: collector sample id
FIELD.NAME: collector_sample_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The living, culturable, named laboratory strain which the sequenced material is derived from as identifed by the collector. { examples: ["NHMUK015621992"] }

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection: core sample properties

FIELD.LABEL: sample derived from
FIELD.NAME: sample_derived_from
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reference to parental sample(s) or original run(s) that the assembly is derived from. The referenced samples or runs should already be registered in INSDC. This should be formatted as one of the following. A single sample/run e.g. ERSxxxxxx OR a comma separated list e.g. ERSxxxxxx,ERSxxxxxx OR a range e.g. ERSxxxxxx-ERSxxxxxx

FIELD.LABEL: sample same as
FIELD.NAME: sample_same_as
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reference to sample(s) that are equivalent. The referenced sample(s) should already be registered in INSDC. This should be formatted as one of the following. A single sample e.g. ERSxxxxxx OR a comma separated list e.g. ERSxxxxxx,ERSxxxxxx

FIELD.LABEL: area sampled size
FIELD.NAME: area_sampled_size
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The total amount or size (volume (ml), mass (g) or area (m2) ) of sample collected.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample processing

FIELD.LABEL: sample material processing
FIELD.NAME: sample_material_processing
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A brief description of any processing applied to the sample during or after retrieving the sample from environment, or a link to the relevant protocol(s) performed.

FIELD.LABEL: preservation date
FIELD.NAME: preservation_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The date when the sample was preserved. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid ISO8601 compliant times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008.

FIELD.LABEL: preservative added to sample
FIELD.NAME: preservative_added_to_sample
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Preservative added to the sample (e.g. Rnalater, alcohol, formaldehyde, etc.). Where appropriate include volume added (e.g. Rnalater; 2 ml)

FIELD.LABEL: preserved by
FIELD.NAME: preserved_by
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of persons that carried out the preservation (including specimen dissection and tissue removal).

FIELD.LABEL: voucher type
FIELD.NAME: voucher_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Status of the specimen in an accessioning process. Examples include: 'Museum Vouchered:Type', 'Museum Vouchered:Type Series', 'Vouchered:Registered Collection', 'E-Vouchered:DNA/Tissue+Photo', 'No specimen'

FIELD.LABEL: preserver affiliation
FIELD.NAME: preserver_affiliation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The university, institution, or society responsible for the collected specimen preservation. This is typically the society or institution of the person(s) specified in the "preserved by" field.

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid amplification
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_amplification
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the enzymatic amplification (PCR, TMA, NASBA) of specific nucleic acids

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid extraction
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_extraction
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the material separation to recover the nucleic acid fraction from a sample

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid extraction date
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_extraction_date
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The date when the nucleic acid extraction was started from the sample material. In case no exact time is available, the date can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008; Except: 2008-01; 2008 all are ISO8601 compliant

FIELD.LABEL: nucleic acid extraction kit
FIELD.NAME: nucleic_acid_extraction_kit
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The name of the extraction kit used to recover the nucleic acid fraction of an input material is performed.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Organism characteristics: ecosystem

FIELD.LABEL: sample symbiont of
FIELD.NAME: sample_symbiont_of
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Reference to host sample from symbiont. The referenced sample should already be registered in INSDC. E.g. ERSxxxxxx

FIELD.LABEL: propagation
FIELD.NAME: propagation
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The type of reproduction from the parent stock. Values for this field is specific to different taxa. For phage or virus: lytic/lysogenic/temperate/obligately lytic. For plasmids: incompatibility group. For eukaryotes: sexual/asexual. Mandatory for MIGs of eukayotes, plasmids and viruses.

FIELD.LABEL: relationship
FIELD.NAME: relationship
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: indicates if the specimen has a known relationship to another specimen (e.g. parental, child, sibling or other kind of relationship)

FIELD.LABEL: relationship to oxygen
FIELD.NAME: relationship_to_oxygen
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Is this organism an aerobe, anaerobe? Please note that aerobic and anaerobic are valid descriptors for microbial environments

FIELD.LABEL: observed biotic relationship
FIELD.NAME: observed_biotic_relationship
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Description of relationship(s) between the subject organism and other organism(s) it is associated with. E.g., parasite on species X; mutualist with species Y. The target organism is the subject of the relationship, and the other organism(s) is the object.

FIELD.LABEL: trophic level
FIELD.NAME: trophic_level
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Trophic levels are the feeding position in a food chain. Microbes can be a range of producers (e.g. chemolithotroph)

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Infraspecies information

FIELD.LABEL: culture_or_strain_id
FIELD.NAME: culture_or_strain_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: living, culturable, named laboratory strain that sequenced material is derived from.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Environmental information

FIELD.LABEL: depth
FIELD.NAME: depth
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The vertical distance below local surface, e.g. for sediment or soil samples depth is measured from sediment or soil surface, respectively. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples.

TEXT_FIELD.REGEX_VALUE: ((0|((0\.)|([1-9][0-9]*\.?))[0-9]*)([Ee][+-]?[0-9]+)?)|((^not applicable$)|(^not collected$)|(^not provided$)|(^restricted access$)|(^missing: control sample$)|(^missing: sample group$)|(^missing: synthetic construct$)|(^missing: lab stock$)|(^missing: third party data$)|(^missing: data agreement established pre-2023$)|(^missing: endangered species$)|(^missing: human-identifiable$)|(^missing$))

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: concentration measurement

FIELD.LABEL: salinity
FIELD.NAME: salinity
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The total concentration of all dissolved salts in a liquid or solid sample. While salinity can be measured by a complete chemical analysis, this method is difficult and time consuming. More often, it is instead derived from the conductivity measurement. This is known as practical salinity. These derivations compare the specific conductance of the sample to a salinity standard such as seawater.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: sample collection

FIELD.LABEL: amount or size of sample collected
FIELD.NAME: amount_or_size_of_sample_collected
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The total amount or size (volume (ml), mass (g) or area (m2) ) of sample collected.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Pointer to physical material

FIELD.LABEL: specimen_voucher
FIELD.NAME: specimen_voucher
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Unique identifier that references the physical specimen that remains after the sequence has been obtained and that ideally exists in a curated collection. The ID should have the following structure: name of the institution (institution code) followed by the collection code (if available) and the voucher id (institution_code:collection_code:voucher_id). Please note institution codes and collection codes are taken from a controlled vocabulary maintained by the INSDC: https://ftp.ncbi.nih.gov/pub/taxonomy/biocollections/

FIELD.LABEL: bio_material
FIELD.NAME: bio_material
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Unique identifier that references the biological material from which the sample was obtained and that ideally exists in a curated collection (e.g. stock centres, seed banks, DNA banks). The ID should have the following structure: name of the institution (institution code) followed by the collection code (if available) and the voucher id (institution_code:collection_code:voucher_id). Please note institution codes and collection codes are taken from a controlled vocabulary maintained by the INSDC: https://ftp.ncbi.nih.gov/pub/taxonomy/biocollections/

FIELD.LABEL: source material identifiers
FIELD.NAME: source_material_identifiers
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A unique identifier assigned to a material sample (as defined by http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/materialSampleID, and as opposed to a particular digital record of a material sample) used for extracting nucleic acids, and subsequent sequencing. The identifier can refer either to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples. The INSDC qualifiers /specimen_voucher, /bio_material, or /culture_collection may or may not share the same value as the source_mat_id field. For instance, the /specimen_voucher qualifier and source_mat_id may both contain 'UAM:Herps:14' , referring to both the specimen voucher and sampled tissue with the same identifier. However, the /culture_collection qualifier may refer to a value from an initial culture (e.g. ATCC:11775) while source_mat_id would refer to an identifier from some derived culture from which the nucleic acids were extracted (e.g. xatc123 or ark:/2154/R2).

FIELD.LABEL: proxy biomaterial
FIELD.NAME: proxy_biomaterial
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: For use if voucher material needs to be made from a material that is different from the one submitted for sequencing. Please record the unique identifier that references the biomaterial and that ideally exists in a curated collection (e.g. stock centres, seed banks, DNA banks). The ID should have the following structure: name of the institution (institution code) followed by the collection code (if available) and the material id (institution_code:collection_code:material_id). Please note institution codes and collection codes are taken from a controlled vocabulary maintained by the INSDC: https://ftp.ncbi.nih.gov/pub/taxonomy/biocollections/

FIELD.LABEL: proxy voucher
FIELD.NAME: proxy_voucher
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: For use if voucher material needs to be made from a specimen that is different than the one submitted for sequencing. Please record the unique identifier that references the physical specimen and that ideally exists in a curated collection. The ID should have the following structure: name of the institution (institution code) followed by the collection code (if available) and the voucher id (institution_code:collection_code:voucher_id). Please note institution codes and collection codes are taken from a controlled vocabulary maintained by the INSDC: https://ftp.ncbi.nih.gov/pub/taxonomy/biocollections/

FIELD.LABEL: specimen_id
FIELD.NAME: specimen_id
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Unique identifier used to link all data for the recorded specimen.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: non-sample terms

FIELD.LABEL: relevant standard operating procedures
FIELD.NAME: relevant_standard_operating_procedures
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Standard operating procedures used in assembly and/or annotation of genomes, metagenomes or environmental sequences in the format of a PMID, DOI or URL

FIELD.LABEL: extrachromosomal elements
FIELD.NAME: extrachromosomal_elements
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Do plasmids exist of significant phenotypic consequence (e.g. ones that determine virulence or antibiotic resistance). Megaplasmids? Other plasmids (borrelia has 15+ plasmids).

FIELD.LABEL: DNA concentration
FIELD.NAME: dna_concentration
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: DNA concentration used for sequencing in ng/µl

FIELD.LABEL: instrument for DNA concentration measurement
FIELD.NAME: instrument_for_dna_concentration_measurement
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name/type of instrument used to measure the DNA concentration (ng/µl) prior to library preparation

FIELD.LABEL: barcoding center
FIELD.NAME: barcoding_center
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Center where DNA barcoding was/will be performed.

FIELD.LABEL: relevant electronic resources
FIELD.NAME: relevant_electronic_resources
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A related resource that is referenced, cited, or otherwise associated to the sequence in the format of a PMID, DOI or URL

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Part and developmental stage of organism

FIELD.LABEL: lifestage
FIELD.NAME: lifestage
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: the age class or life stage of the organism at the time of collection.

FIELD.LABEL: organism part
FIELD.NAME: organism_part
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The part of organism's anatomy or substance arising from an organism from which the biomaterial was derived, excludes cells.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: experimental factor and block

FIELD.LABEL: negative control type
FIELD.NAME: negative_control_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The substance or equipment used as a negative control in an investigation

FIELD.LABEL: positive control type
FIELD.NAME: positive_control_type
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The substance, mixture, product, or apparatus used to verify that a process which is part of an investigation delivers a true positive.

FIELD.LABEL: experimental factor
FIELD.NAME: experimental_factor
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Experimental factors are essentially the variable aspects of an experiment design which can be used to describe an experiment, or set of experiments, in an increasingly detailed manner. This field accepts ontology terms from Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO) and/or Ontology for Biomedical Investigations (OBI). For a browser of EFO (v 2.95) terms, please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/EFO; for a browser of OBI (v 2018-02-12) terms please see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/OBI. E.g. time series design [EFO:EFO_0001779]

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: non-sample terms: study or project

FIELD.LABEL: project name
FIELD.NAME: project_name
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Name of the project within which the sequencing was organized

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: ancient DNA specific

FIELD.LABEL: preservational treatment
FIELD.NAME: preservational_treatment
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Description of any treatment applied to samples for the purpose of maximising collection preservation that may influence downstream DNA recovery or library construction, such as storage fluid or reconstructive glue

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Organism characteristics: genetic

FIELD.LABEL: subspecific genetic lineage
FIELD.NAME: subspecific_genetic_lineage
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Information about the genetic distinctness of the sequenced organism below the subspecies level, e.g., serovar, serotype, biotype, ecotype, or any relevant genetic typing schemes like Group I plasmid. Subspecies should not be recorded in this term, but in the NCBI taxonomy. Supply both the lineage name and the lineage rank separated by a colon, e.g., biovar:abc123.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Marine Event

FIELD.LABEL: event label
FIELD.NAME: event_label
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: An Event corresponds to the deployment of a sampling device. It is bound in space and time. The Event Label is a unique identifier composed of the project prefix "TARA", sampling date/time "yyyymmddThh:mm:ssZ", station number "000" and a short label to indicate the sampling device used, e.g. "EVENT_CAST", "EVENT_NET", "EVENT_PUMP". Example: TARA_20110416T1508Z_100_EVENT_CAST.

FIELD_GROUP.restrictionType: Any number or none of the fields
FIELD_GROUP.NAME: Reference

FIELD.LABEL: isolation and growth condition
FIELD.NAME: isolation_and_growth_condition
FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Publication reference in the form of pubmed ID (pmid), digital object identifier (doi) or url for isolation and growth condition specifications of the organism/material. Mandatory for MIGS and MIMARKS Specimen.
